articles.title: Plan to scrap presidential elections puts Zimbabweans at loggerheads
articles.description: The ruling party has unveiled a draft law that strips voters of the chance to elect the president.

more.title: How the Skeleton of Mutapa Times is Based on Sarah Charlesworth's Work
more.description: Conceptual artist Sarah Charlesworth's 1979 study of how newspapers covered a solar eclipse inspired the framework behind The Mutapa Times.

more.title: AC Milan booed off after shock home loss to Udinese and Juventus closes the gap
more.description: MILAN — After falling out of the Serie A title race, AC Milan is looking nervously at the teams behind it following a shock 3-0 loss at home to Udinese on Saturday. more.title: Bayern warms up for Madrid by smashing Bundesliga goal record and closing in on title
more.description: BERLIN — Bayern Munich warmed up for Real Madrid’s visit by smashing the Bundesliga record for goals in a season and taking a major step toward yet another league title on Saturday. Leon Goretzka scored Bayern’s record-breaking 102nd league goal of the season as relegation-threatened St. Pauli was routed 5-0 away. more.title: Arsenal falls to Bournemouth and lets Man City back into Premier League title race
more.description: Arsenal’s Premier League title bid was hit by a stunning 2-1 loss to Bournemouth at Emirates Stadium on Saturday. more.title: Iran War Drives US Inflation Spike, Consumer Confidence Slumps — Report
more.description: WASHINGTON — The ongoing conflict in Iran has triggered a sharp surge in inflation in the United States, driven largely by a dramatic rise in fuel prices, according to a report by the Associated Press. more.title: Unifreight Africa Targets Tobacco Haulage Expansion
more.description: HARARE – Unifreight Africa says it will ramp up fleet capacity and operational efficiency in 2026 as it targets transporting more than 100 million kilogrammes of tobacco, up from 84 million kilogrammes handled in 2025. more.title: DR Congo central bank to ban US dollar use
more.description: The Democratic Republic of Congo’s central bank said Thursday that it would ban the use of dollars and other foreign currencies for cash transactions in the country. Foreign currencies have gradually overtaken the use of the weak local Congo franc in recent years in stores and other places. more.title: Reimagining Zimbabwe’s Digital Future: Delivering End-to-End, Tech-Enabled Transformation in a Constrained but Opportunity-Rich Economy
more.description: Zimbabwe stands at a pivotal inflexion point in its digital evolution.
